notes_md:
**Sung sướng** means *very happy, delighted, overjoyed* — stronger than just “happy.” It’s a warm, emotional kind of happiness, often after good news or something you really wanted happens.

It’s an **adjective**, so it describes how someone feels. You usually use it with *cảm thấy / thấy* or after a subject: *tôi sung sướng*, *cảm thấy sung sướng*.

It’s stronger than **vui** (happy/fun). If *vui* is normal happy, *sung sướng* is “so happy I can feel it.”  

Opposite: **buồn** (sad), **thất vọng** (disappointed).
exampleSentencesTranslated: He feels very happy when he receives good news from his family.
She is always happy whenever she travels with her friends.
I was very happy when I got a high score in the recent exam.
notes:
Sung sướng means very happy, delighted, overjoyed — stronger than just “happy.” It’s a warm, emotional kind of happiness, often after good news or something you really wanted happens.

It’s an adjective, so it describes how someone feels. You usually use it with cảm thấy / thấy or after a subject: tôi sung sướng, cảm thấy sung sướng.

It’s stronger than vui (happy/fun). If vui is normal happy, sung sướng is “so happy I can feel it.”  

Opposite: buồn (sad), thất vọng (disappointed).
exampleSentences: Anh cảm thấy rất sung sướng khi nhận được tin tốt từ gia đình.
Chị ấy luôn sung sướng mỗi khi đi du lịch với bạn bè.
Em đã rất sung sướng khi đạt điểm cao trong kỳ thi vừa rồi.
